description_long: This object is a heavy-duty pick hammer featuring a robust, dark wooden handle and a large, double-sided metal head. The head is forged from a gray, slightly textured metal and is shaped with two pointed, opposing ends, ideal for breaking or prying hard surfaces such as concrete or stone. The handle is cylindrical, straight, and appears to be made from solid wood, providing a sturdy grip for heavy impacts. The metal head is securely mounted atop the handle, with no visible fasteners or additional attachments. The overall design is utilitarian, with no decorative elements or branding visible. The hammer appears well-crafted and suitable for demanding urban construction or demolition tasks.
description: A heavy-duty pick hammer with a solid wooden handle and a double-pointed gray metal head, designed for breaking or prying hard surfaces. The tool is robust and utilitarian, with no visible branding or attachments.
description_view_0: Front view shows the cylindrical wooden handle and the narrow profile of the metal head, with both pointed ends aligned horizontally.
description_view_1: Side view reveals the full span of the double-pointed metal head, highlighting its symmetrical, elongated shape and the sturdy attachment to the handle.
description_view_2: Rear view mirrors the front, displaying the handle and the narrow edge of the metal head, with the pointed ends visible on either side.
description_view_3: Opposite side view again shows the full width of the metal head, emphasizing its length and the robust connection to the wooden handle.
attribute_handle_type: straight wooden handle, cylindrical, smooth surface, no visible grip enhancements
attribute_head_type: double-pointed pick head, forged steel, symmetrical with two elongated pointed ends, no claws or striking faces
